narratives.title: Macro Narrative
narratives.body: Macro sentiment is mixed rather than risk-off. The active seven-day tape holds 7 high-signal rows and the policy-uncertainty index cooled again to 221.01, so macro fear has stepped back even while oil volatility stays sticky.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Classify macro sentiment pressure so buyers can judge timing risk before conviction appears in price structure.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: Narrative pressure becomes operationally relevant when volatility and positioning align with it.
quantResearchProtocol: Track narrative persistence and pressure concentration across recent windows.
Classify regime as stable, mixed, stretched, or unstable.
Cross-check regime against volatility context and positioning state.
Translate regime into buyer timing implications.
inferenceConstraints: Do not infer regime shifts from isolated stories.
Do not use hype language or trader slang.
Do not omit confidence caveats when evidence is thin.
outputRequirements: State sentiment regime and persistence horizon.
Report procurement timing implication with uncertainty clarity.

sourceRecords.recordHint: latest seven-day macro and policy narrative rows

notes: Macro narrative is anchored to the live uncertainty series plus the active headline window.
The card explicitly separates a series shock from headline-density breadth.

narratives.title: Flow Narrative
narratives.body: Flow is narrow, not broad. The current seven-day pull carries 7 rows, with only 1 energy-tagged row, 1 tariff-tagged row, and zero China-tagged soy rows, so conviction still depends more on oil volatility than headline breadth.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Determine whether current participation flow is stable, crowded, or fragile for procurement timing decisions.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: Crowded or unstable flow increases reversal risk and decision-latency cost for buyers.
quantResearchProtocol: Measure flow persistence versus burstiness in recent sessions.
Map flow state to sentiment drift and volatility context.
Flag conviction fragility when channels conflict.
Express impact in buyer execution-risk language.
inferenceConstraints: Do not infer conviction from one-day flow bursts.
Do not overfit flow to direction without regime context.
Do not suppress uncertainty when flow signals conflict.
outputRequirements: Classify flow state with confidence qualifier.
Explain likely impact on buyer timing and slippage risk.

sourceRecords.recordHint: latest seven-day sentiment rows and tag counts

notes: Flow narrative is density-based rather than hype-based.
Concentrated narrative breadth keeps the cadence tight without pretending the tape is broad.

narratives.title: Procurement Narrative
narratives.body: Procurement psychology should stay disciplined, not reactive. Managed money is still bullish and net long 158,107 contracts, but the live driver stack averages only 37.0, so Chris should keep staged coverage and treat crude rebounds as the trigger to re-check, not as proof the whole regime changed.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Convert sentiment and positioning conditions into buyer-ready pacing guidance under uncertainty.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: Explicit pacing rules reduce emotional overreaction and timing regret during unstable sentiment regimes.
quantResearchProtocol: Combine sentiment score, flow state, and macro regime into a buyer posture.
Score decision-latency risk versus over-hedge risk under current regime.
Align guidance to staged-execution logic when channel correlation is high.
State invalidation conditions for posture changes.
inferenceConstraints: Do not recommend static cadence in elevated mixed-risk states.
Do not issue binary buy-or-wait calls without uncertainty framing.
Do not ignore asymmetric downside scenarios.

sourceRecords.recordHint: current average risk-network score and policy leadership

notes: Procurement narrative combines positioning with network pressure instead of treating either one as sufficient by itself.

positioningFlow.title: Managed Money Positioning
positioningFlow.body: Managed-money positioning is still bullish, with a 158,107-contract net long equal to 22.08% of open interest as of 2026-05-19. That is enough length to accelerate upside buyer pain if crude rebounds, but it is still a weekly context signal, not intraday truth.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Determine whether managed-money positioning is stabilizing or destabilizing soybean-oil procurement expectations.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: Positioning concentration can amplify reflex moves before fundamentals fully confirm.
quantResearchProtocol: Read latest managed-money net and ratio to open interest.
Classify bias with freshness caveat because managed-money positioning updates weekly.
Cross-map bias against current volatility and headline pressure.
Translate result into buyer caution level.
inferenceConstraints: Do not treat weekly managed-money positioning as real-time certainty.
Do not equate size with durability of conviction.
outputRequirements: State bias class with confidence caveat.

notes: Bias classification is weekly and must be read with freshness discipline.
Output is buyer-facing, not speculative positioning advice.

headlineFlow.title: Headline Flow
headlineFlow.body: Headline velocity still matters because the tape is small enough to inspect: 7 active rows, 1 tariff-tagged item, and no China-tagged soy rows. That is signal, not noise, but it is still too concentrated to support a broad-based panic narrative.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Assess whether headline stream is producing actionable signal or noise for procurement decisions.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: Headline velocity becomes actionable when thematic coherence and persistence reinforce live market pressure.
quantResearchProtocol: Track 24-hour and seven-day headline density.
Identify whether tape is energy-led, policy-led, or mixed.
Compare headline concentration with live market drivers.
Set monitoring cadence from coherence, not count alone.
inferenceConstraints: Do not infer regime shift from velocity alone.
Do not ignore low thematic density.
Do not provide cadence advice without coherence checks.
outputRequirements: State whether velocity is signal or noise.

sourceRecords.recordHint: Farm, Food, and National Security Act current watch item

notes: Headline-flow framing gives more weight to thematic coherence than raw count.
The tape is still too concentrated for regime-calling on headlines alone.
